default: Roll 2D for each of the six characteristics (Str, Dex, End, Int, Edu, Soc).
Choose a service or take random; the enlistment roll determines acceptance vs. draft.
Each four-year term: survival, commission and promotion, skills, then aging from age 34+. Reenlistment at term end.
Muster out: cash and material benefits (passages, weapons, ships, TAS) determined by rank, terms, and rolls.

edition.name: Classic Traveller
edition.description: Classic Traveller: six services (Navy/Marines/Army/Scouts/Merchants/Other) plus Citizens of the Imperium expansions. Roll, enlist, run four-year terms, muster out. The original 1981 chargen.

title: Classic Traveller character generation data
extractionNotes: Extracted from the two local PDF manuals only: CT_TTB_The_Traveller_Book.pdf and Classic_Traveller_CT_S04_Supplement_04_Citizens_of_the_Imperium.pdf.
Table cells printed as dashes are stored as null, except cash tables where a dash is stored as 0 for numeric consumption.
Attribute names are normalized to strength, dexterity, endurance, intelligence, education, and social while table labels preserve the book wording where useful.
CotI uses Position for the commission-equivalent roll. TTB uses Commission. Both are stored as checks.position with a label.
Book page references are printed page numbers, not PDF page indexes.

cotiDifferences: CotI has no draft table; a failed enlistment means the character tries another career.
CotI belter and barbarian careers begin at age 14 instead of age 18.
CotI uses Position as the commission-equivalent rank-entry roll.
CotI nobles are automatic only for social standing 10+.
CotI bureaucrats invert re-enlistment: they must roll the target or higher to leave before retirement.

sheet.$comment: Presentation metadata (TAS Form 2 box 17 'Equipment Qualified On'), not a game rule: equipment-operation skills listed individually; lib/pdfSheet.ts unions in the vehicle cascade (+Includes expansion).

skillTableMeta.$comment: TTB Acquired Skills tables (Book 1 / CotI). Same four-table layout as MT.

nobleTitles.$rule: TTB p. 25 NOBLE RANKS: Social Standing B-F (11-15) confers a noble title (Knight/Sir, Baron, Marquis, Count, Duke). CotI p. 8 lists the rank letters B-F but defers the title names to Book 3 (TTB p. 25).

attributeCaps.$rule: TTB p. 17: 'For player characters the values of characteristics may not exceed 15; values do not go below 1 except for the results of calamitous injury or aging.'

skillEligibility.notes: Only Scouts (TTB p.24) gain two skills per term. All others (including the rankless CotI services: Belters, Doctors, Rogues, Scientists, Hunters) gain two in the first term and one per term thereafter (CotI p.3); rankless careers simply forgo the position/promotion skill bonuses.

musterOutRolls.notes: TTB p.24: one roll per full term plus a rank-band bonus (rank 1-2 +1, 3-4 +2, 5-6 +3) for Book 1 services. CotI p.4/6/8: Supplement 4 careers get +2 (not +3) at rank 5-6 via rankExtraRollsBySource.coti, selected by each service's source. Cash-table DM +1 for Gambling-1+ (TTB p.29 / CotI) or, for CotI careers, for having retired (CotI p.6/8) - not cumulative: cashTableDmCumulative:false makes cashDmFor take the single largest matching +1 rather than summing.

reenlistment.$rule: TTB p. 24 (Prior Service Table reenlist throws 6/6/7/3/4/5), pp. 18-19. mandatoryOnExactRoll: a reenlistment throw of exactly 12 forces another term regardless of player preference (p. 18). mandatoryRetireAfterTerm: 'A character may serve up to seven terms of service voluntarily' (p. 19); after the 7th term retirement is mandatory UNLESS a mandatory reenlistment (natural 12) forces more (p. 19). Precedence: the natural-12 mandatory reenlist is therefore evaluated before the term-7 retirement cap.

survival.$rule: TTB p. 18: 'Upon enlistment (or upon being drafted), a character embarks on a term of service lasting four years.' Survival failure is death: TTB p. 18 'Failure to achieve the survival throw means the character dies' (the CotI optional injury/short-term rule is not modeled as a lifecycle default).

draft.$rule: TTB p. 18: 'Draftees are not eligible for commissions during their first term of service; they do become eligible during the second and subsequent terms of service if they reenlist.'

retirement.$rule: Retirement eligible after 5 completed terms (TTB p. 19); pension Cr4000/yr at 5 terms + Cr2000/yr per further term = Cr4000+(terms-5)*2000, matching the printed 4000/6000/8000/10000/12000 table (TTB p. 25). Scouts and Other cannot retire (TTB p. 29); Barbarians cannot retire (CotI p. 8).

agingCrisis.$ruleRestoreTo: TTB p. 24 (aging crisis) + TTB p. 17 characteristic floor ('values do not go below 1 except through calamitous injury or aging'): a survivor's reduced characteristic is restored to 1.

pdfExtraction.imageVerification: pdftoppm at 600–700 DPI for cell-by-cell verification of disputed entries
pdfExtraction.canonicalTextLocation: docs/canonical/ — pdftotext outputs of each chargen page checked into the repo for audit
auditFindings: Scouts Personal Development row 6 was +1 Social in the JS source; TTB p. 25 prints Gun Cbt (confirmed via 600 DPI render). Fixed.
Marines rank 3 was 'Force Comdr' in the JS; TTB p. 25 prints 'Force Cmdr'. Fixed.
Diplomats ranks 1-2 were '3rd Secretary'/'2nd Secretary' in the JS; CotI p. 11 prints '3d Secretary'/'2d Secretary'. Fixed.

attributeAbbreviations.$comment: TTB skill-table abbreviations (same set as MT).

skillLabelRenames.$comment: TTB acquired-skills tables print 'Engnrng', 'Electronics', 'Fwd Obsv' as abbreviations; engine uses 'Engineering', 'Electronic', 'Fwd Obsvr'.

cascadeAliases.$comment: Printed cell label (lowercased) → cascade JSON key. CT uses the same aliases as MT — labels are stable across editions even when the cascade pools differ.

$ruleWeaponBenefitTypes: CT/CotI Weapon benefit: the generic 'Weapon' mustering-out benefit is resolved as a two-stage pick — first the weapon type (Blade or Gun), then a specific weapon from that type's cascade.
$musterBenefitDefinitions: Mustering-out benefit meanings, one clause each, condensed from The Traveller Book pp. 29-30 (passages, Travellers' Aid, weapons, starships) and Citizens of the Imperium pp. 13-15 (Weapon, Instruments, Watch, ship descriptions). Keyed to the benefit token reaching cellResolver's logMusterBenefitMeaning.

musterBenefitDefinitions.Weapon: any one personal weapon (blade or gun) from Book 1; a repeat may instead raise skill in it
musterBenefitDefinitions.Blade: any blade weapon of choice; a repeat gives another blade or raises blade skill
musterBenefitDefinitions.Gun: any gun weapon of choice; a repeat gives another gun or raises gun skill
musterBenefitDefinitions.TAS: Travellers' Aid Society membership; hostels at class A/B starports and a High Passage every two months, once per character
musterBenefitDefinitions.High Passage: a first-class starship ticket; excellent cuisine and one ton of baggage (about Cr10,000)
musterBenefitDefinitions.Mid Passage: a second-class starship ticket; passable fare, 100 kg baggage, bumpable if a high passenger appears (about Cr8,000)
musterBenefitDefinitions.Low Passage: a cryogenic cold-sleep starship ticket; the traveller is frozen and risks death on revival (about Cr1,000)
musterBenefitDefinitions.Free Trader: a Type A free trader, a 200-ton cargo ship carrying a forty-year mortgage
musterBenefitDefinitions.Scout Ship: a Type S scout/courier on loan from the Scout Service; cannot be sold or mortgaged
musterBenefitDefinitions.Corsair: a Type P corsair, an armed pirate raiding ship held outright
musterBenefitDefinitions.Seeker: a Type J seeker, a small asteroid mining and prospecting ship
musterBenefitDefinitions.Yacht: a yacht received as a benefit; the starship is detailed in Book 1
musterBenefitDefinitions.Lab Ship: a Type L laboratory ship supplied by a scientific foundation; cannot be sold
musterBenefitDefinitions.Safari Ship: a Type K safari ship with animal caging areas and a trophy lounge
musterBenefitDefinitions.Instruments: a complete medical instrument set — surgeon's tools, diagnostics and drugs (about Cr5,000)
musterBenefitDefinitions.Watch: a presentation watch from fellow bureaucrats; about Cr50, occasionally worth Cr200-1200

$positionDefinitions: Position/commission meanings, keyed by the service's checks.position.label: 'Commission' from The Traveller Book p. 18 (Commissions and Promotions), 'Position' from Citizens of the Imperium p. 3 (Positions and Promotions).

positionDefinitions.Commission: an officer's commission; a successful throw grants rank 1, attempted once per term until achieved
positionDefinitions.Position: the rank-entry roll — a commission in military careers, other meanings elsewhere; success grants rank 1

$skillDefinitions: Skill meanings, one clause each, condensed from The Traveller Book pp. 21-28 (Available Skills descriptions, incl. the Blades/Polearms and Guns tables p. 25) and Citizens of the Imperium pp. 10-11, 15-18 (weapon expertise, bow weapons, Air Craft, Hunting, Prospecting, Vehicle, Water Craft). Keyed to the post-rename name Character.addSkill receives; cascade picks are glossed individually. Skills whose only Traveller definition lives in Book 4 (Mercenary) or Book 5 (High Guard) — Battle Dress, Carousing, Commo, Demolition, Gravitics, Instruction, Interrogation, Liaison, Recon, Recruiting, Ship Tactic, Ship Tactics, Survival, Zero-G Cbt — carry no TTB/CotI definition and are omitted (fail-soft).

skillDefinitions.Admin: experience dealing with and managing bureaucratic agencies and their officials
skillDefinitions.Air/Raft: trained in the air/raft, floater, flier and all grav vehicles
skillDefinitions.ATV: trained in all-terrain vehicles, both wheeled and tracked
skillDefinitions.Brawling: hand-to-hand fighting with fists, clubs, bottles and improvised weapons
skillDefinitions.Bribery: experience bribing officials to circumvent regulations or ignore laws
skillDefinitions.Computer: programming and operating computers, both ground and shipboard
skillDefinitions.Electronic: using, operating and repairing electronic devices, including energy weapons
skillDefinitions.Engineering: operating and maintaining starship maneuver drives, jump drives and power plants
skillDefinitions.Forgery: faking documents and papers to deceive officials, banks or patrons
skillDefinitions.Fwd Obsvr: trained to call and adjust artillery, missile and laser fire from distant batteries
skillDefinitions.Gambling: well informed on games of chance and skilled in their play
skillDefinitions.Gunnery: operating turret weapons mounted aboard spacecraft — lasers, sandcasters, missiles
skillDefinitions.Hunting: skilled in tracking and hunting animals
skillDefinitions.Jack-o-T: a resourceful all-rounder; confers level-0 in every other skill
skillDefinitions.Leader: able to command and lead groups of soldiers or hirelings
skillDefinitions.Mechanical: using, operating and repairing mechanical (non-energy) devices
skillDefinitions.Medical: training and skill in the medical arts and sciences
skillDefinitions.Navigation: the art and science of interplanetary and interstellar navigation
skillDefinitions.Pilot: operating starships and large interplanetary ships
skillDefinitions.Prospecting: experienced in searching out mineral deposits on worlds and in deep space
skillDefinitions.Ship's Boat: operating small interplanetary craft — shuttles, launches, lifeboats, fighters
skillDefinitions.Steward: the care and feeding of a starship's passengers
skillDefinitions.Streetwise: acquainted with local subcultures; deals with strangers without alienating them
skillDefinitions.Tactics: training in small-unit tactics, up to 1000 troops or single ships
skillDefinitions.Vacc Suit: trained in the vacuum suit, including armored battle dress
skillDefinitions.Dagger: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the dagger
skillDefinitions.Blade: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the blade (the default edged weapon)
skillDefinitions.Foil: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the foil
skillDefinitions.Sword: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the sword
skillDefinitions.Cutlass: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the cutlass
skillDefinitions.Broadsword: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the broadsword
skillDefinitions.Bayonet: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the bayonet
skillDefinitions.Spear: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the spear
skillDefinitions.Halberd: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the halberd
skillDefinitions.Pike: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the pike
skillDefinitions.Cudgel: Blade Combat expertise, taken as the cudgel
skillDefinitions.Body Pistol: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the body pistol
skillDefinitions.Auto Pistol: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the automatic pistol
skillDefinitions.Revolver: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the revolver
skillDefinitions.Carbine: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the carbine
skillDefinitions.Rifle: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the rifle
skillDefinitions.Auto Rifle: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the automatic rifle
skillDefinitions.Shotgun: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the shotgun
skillDefinitions.SMG: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the submachine gun
skillDefinitions.Laser Carbine: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the laser carbine
skillDefinitions.Laser Rifle: Gun Combat expertise, taken as the laser rifle
skillDefinitions.Sling: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the sling
skillDefinitions.Short Bow: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the short bow
skillDefinitions.Long Bow: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the long bow
skillDefinitions.Sporting Crossbow: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the sporting crossbow
skillDefinitions.Military Crossbow: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the military crossbow
skillDefinitions.Repeating Crossbow: Bow Combat expertise, taken as the repeating crossbow
skillDefinitions.Prop-driven Fixed Wing: Vehicle skill piloting propeller-driven fixed-wing aircraft
skillDefinitions.Jet-driven Fixed Wing: Vehicle skill piloting jet-driven fixed-wing aircraft
skillDefinitions.Grav Vehicle: Vehicle skill operating grav vehicles (equivalent to Air/Raft)
skillDefinitions.Wheeled Vehicle: Vehicle skill operating wheeled vehicles (equivalent to ATV)
skillDefinitions.Small Watercraft: Vehicle skill operating small watercraft, under 50 tons

$characteristicDefinitions: The Traveller Book p. 17 (Characters — Characteristics), condensed to one clause each.

characteristicDefinitions.name: Strength
characteristicDefinitions.meaning: a general measure of physical power, and the force applicable in combat

characteristicDefinitions.name: Education
characteristicDefinitions.meaning: the highest level of schooling the individual has attained

characteristicDefinitions.name: Social Standing
characteristicDefinitions.meaning: the social class and level of society the character's family comes from

$ruleServiceOrder: TTB p. 18 enlistment/draft order: the service-selection table lists Navy, Marines, Army, Scouts, Merchants, Other before the Advanced Character Generation services; nobles are auto-enrolled (Soc 10+, CotI) and never voluntarily enlisted.
$serviceDescriptions: Per-service `description` blurbs. The 12 CotI services (pirates, belters, sailors, diplomats, doctors, flyers, barbarians, bureaucrats, rogues, nobles, scientists, hunters) are lifted verbatim from Citizens of the Imperium p. 5 'THE CHARACTER TYPES'. The 6 core TTB services (navy, marines, army, scouts, merchants, other) carry engine-inferred (basis B) one-liners synthesized from their Prior Service tables on The Traveller Book pp. 24-25 (ranks, skills, draft slot, mustering-out benefits) — TTB prints no per-service narrative — and 'Other' additionally borrows CotI p. 1's residual 'other pursuits' framing. No wording invented.

navy.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (ranks Ensign→Admiral; Ship's Boat/Gunnery/Pilot/Engineering skills; draft 1) (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
navy.description: Interstellar naval service — commissioned officers crewing and fighting starships.

marines.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (Cutlass-1 on joining; ranks Lieutenant→Brigadier) (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
marines.description: Shipborne assault and boarding troops — the navy's ground-combat arm.

army.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (Rifle-1 on joining; ATV/Tactics; ranks Lieutenant→General) (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
army.description: Planetary ground forces — the surface land military.

scouts.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (no ranks; Pilot-1; Scout Ship benefit; 2 skills/term) (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
scouts.description: The exploratory and courier service — rankless pilots surveying frontiers.
notes: Two skills per term; no ranks, commissions, or promotions.

merchants.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (ranks 4th Officer→Captain; Free Trader benefit; Steward skill) (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
merchants.description: The merchant marine — crews of commercial starships trading between worlds.

other.$description: TTB pp. 24-25 Prior Service tables (no ranks; enlist 3+; Streetwise/Gambling/Bribery/Forgery skills) + CotI p. 1 ('no specific set of tables … in other pursuits') (engine-inferred, no printed per-service prose).
other.description: Catch-all for characters outside the formal careers — ordinary and underworld civilians.

pirates.description: Individuals crewing interplanetary or interstellar vessels, who make their living by attacking, hijacking, or plundering commerce.

belters.description: Individuals who prospect and mine asteroid belts in search of mineral deposits, artifacts, or salvage materials.

dms.description: +1 in first term, +2 in second term, through +7 in seventh term

sailors.description: Members of the Nautical Force Command (the wet navy) of a world.

diplomats.description: Members of the Foreign Service of a government.

flyers.description: Members of the Close Orbit and Airspace Control Command (the air force) of a world.

barbarians.description: Rugged individuals from primitive planets accustomed to hardship and well-trained in wilderness and survival situations.

bureaucrats.description: Individuals serving a government or organization in management or executive capacities.

rogues.description: Criminal elements familiar with the rougher or more illegal methods of accomplishing tasks.

nobles.description: Individuals of the upper classes who perform little consistent function, but often have large amounts of ready money.

enlistment.special: Automatic and only available with social standing 10+

rankBySocial.$rule: CotI p. 8: noble starting rank = Social - 10 (Knight B/11 ... Duke F/15), capped at rank 5; Social floored at 10.

nobles.$comment: CotI p. 8 prints no noble automatic skills; the rank<->Social identity (rank = Social-10) is enforced entirely by the noblesSocialByRank doPromotion hook. The former 5x rank +1 Social auto-skills were redundant with that hook (equal result in every reachable state) and are removed; automaticSkills kept as [] so the loader shape holds.
notes: Rank corresponds to noble rank from social standing once position is achieved.

scientists.description: Individuals trained in technological or research sciences who conduct scientific investigations into materials, situations, and phenomena.

hunters.description: Individuals who track and hunt animals of varying sizes and types for profit or enjoyment.
